creator: Sarah Perez
title: (No AI) Anthropic co-founder confirms the company briefed the Trump administration on Mythos
dc:creator: Sarah Perez
content: In an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark explained why the company was still engaged with the U.S. government while simultaneously suing them.
contentSnippet: In an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark explained why the company was still engaged with the U.S. government while simultaneously suing them.

creator: Tim Fernholz
title: (No AI) Max Hodak’s Science Corp. is preparing to place its first sensor in a human brain
dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
content: The device could help address multiple neurological conditions if it proves successful. One early use could be delivering gentle electrical stimulation to damaged brain or spinal cord cells to encourage healing.
contentSnippet: The device could help address multiple neurological conditions if it proves successful. One early use could be delivering gentle electrical stimulation to damaged brain or spinal cord cells to encourage healing.

title: (No AI) The attacks on Sam Altman are a warning for the AI world
author: Lauren Feiner
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/12/STK201_SAM_ALTMAN_CVIRGINIA_C.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Before allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home, the 20-year-old accused attacker wrote about his fear that the AI race would cause humans to go extinct, [the _San Francisco Chronicle_ found](https://www.sfchronicle.com/crime/article/sam-altman-openai-daniel-alejandro-moreno-gama-22201211.php). Two days later, Altman's home appeared to be targeted a second time, according to [_The San Francisco Standard_](https://sfstandard.com/2026/04/12/sam-altman-s-home-targeted-second-attack/). Only a week earlier, an Indianapolis councilman [reported 13 shots fired](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/indianapolis-councilman-says-shots-fired-at-home-and-no-data-centers-note-left-at-door) at his door, with a note that read "No Data Centers," after he'd supported a rezoning petition for a data center developer.

These unsettling incidents have set off alarms in and around the AI industry. There's long been a vocal resistance to the te …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/911778/ai-violence-sam-altman-home)
contentSnippet:
Before allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home, the 20-year-old accused attacker wrote about his fear that the AI race would cause humans to go extinct, the San Francisco Chronicle found. Two days later, Altman's home appeared to be targeted a second time, according to The San Francisco Standard. Only a week earlier, an Indianapolis councilman reported 13 shots fired at his door, with a note that read "No Data Centers," after he'd supported a rezoning petition for a data center developer.
These unsettling incidents have set off alarms in and around the AI industry. There's long been a vocal resistance to the te …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Chrome now lets you turn AI prompts into repeatable ‘Skills’
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![An image of the Chrome logo on a yellow background](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/04/STK114_Google_Chrome_03.webp?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google is launching a new Chrome workflow feature that allows you to reuse your favorite Gemini commands across multiple webpages. Any AI prompts can now be saved as "Skills" in the Chrome desktop browser, letting you instantly run them across any tabs you select.

"Until now, repeating an AI task - like asking for ingredient substitutions to make a recipe vegan - meant re-entering the same prompt as you visited different pages," Chrome product manager Hafsah Ismail said in the announcement. "To make this easier, we're launching Skills in Chrome, which lets you save and reuse your most helpful AI prompts and run them with a single click."

S …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/911658/google-chrome-gemini-ai-skills-availability-launch)

title: (No AI) Trusted access for the next era of cyber defense
content: OpenAI expands its Trusted Access for Cyber program, introducing GPT-5.4-Cyber to vetted defenders and strengthening safeguards as AI cybersecurity capabilities advance.
contentSnippet: OpenAI expands its Trusted Access for Cyber program, introducing GPT-5.4-Cyber to vetted defenders and strengthening safeguards as AI cybersecurity capabilities advance.

title: (No AI) Enterprises power agentic workflows in Cloudflare Agent Cloud with OpenAI

contentSnippet: Cloudflare brings OpenAI’s GPT-5.4 and Codex to Agent Cloud, enabling enterprises to build, deploy, and scale AI agents for real-world tasks with speed and security.

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) How to Use Google Chrome’s New AI-Powered ‘Skills’
dc:creator: Reece Rogers
content: The premade Skills available through the Gemini sidebar in Chrome include ways to maximize protein in recipes or summarize YouTube videos.

creator: Maxwell Zeff
title: (No AI) Anthropic Opposes the Extreme AI Liability Bill That OpenAI Backed
dc:creator: Maxwell Zeff
content: Anthropic and OpenAI are clashing over a proposed Illinois law that would let AI labs largely off the hook for mass deaths and financial disasters.
contentSnippet: Anthropic and OpenAI are clashing over a proposed Illinois law that would let AI labs largely off the hook for mass deaths and financial disasters.
